message: Parameter tuning has stalled. The Gemini agents have been unable to improve the composite score for 3 iterations.

checks.name: temperature_range
checks.reason: Temperatures must stay within physically realizable ocean range

checks.name: equator_pole_gradient
checks.reason: Meridional temperature gradient must exist and be reasonable

checks.name: hemispheric_symmetry
checks.reason: Ocean temperatures are roughly symmetric; large asymmetry suggests broken forcing

checks.name: deep_colder_than_surface
checks.value: 1/15 bands have deep warmer than surface
checks.reason: Deep ocean is always colder than surface (stable stratification)

checks.name: western_intensification
checks.reason: Stommel theory: beta effect concentrates return flow on western boundary

checks.name: subtropical_gyre_exists
checks.reason: Wind-driven subtropical gyre must develop from Sverdrup balance

checks.name: southern_ocean_flow
checks.reason: Drake Passage is open — ACC should develop from wind stress + no land barrier

checks.name: deep_water_formation_signature
checks.reason: Thermohaline circulation: cold dense water sinks at poles, fills deep ocean

recentHistory.hypothesis: Complete collapse of western intensification and AMOC-driven overturning circulation
Complete failure of western boundary current intensification and AMOC-driven overturning circulation.

physicistLastHypothesis.dominant_error: Complete failure of western boundary current intensification and AMOC-driven overturning circulation.

hypotheses.mechanism: The beta-effect or wind-stress curl coupling is failing to generate the necessary vorticity balance for western intensification, likely due to excessive lateral viscosity (A_visc) damping the boundary currents before they can form.
hypotheses.side_effects: Lowering viscosity will likely introduce numerical instability or checkerboard noise in the velocity field, requiring a re-evaluation of the friction (r_friction) to maintain stability.

hypotheses.mechanism: The thermohaline coupling (gamma_deep_form) is too weak to drive the vertical overturning required for the AMOC, causing the deep ocean to remain stagnant and preventing the formation of the western boundary currents that rely on density-driven pressure gradients.
hypotheses.side_effects: Increasing deep formation will likely lead to rapid cooling of the deep ocean and could potentially trigger an ice-albedo runaway if the surface temperature drops too low.

physicistLastHypothesis.recommended: H1 — The western intensification failure is the most glaring structural error; if the boundary currents do not form, the AMOC cannot be sustained regardless of thermodynamic forcing.

suggestion: Consider whether structural changes to the physics code (index.html) are needed — new forcing terms, different boundary conditions, modified advection scheme — rather than just parameter tuning.
